---
title: "Grooming Scenarios"
author: "Anna Willoughby"
date: "2025-05-22"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyr) 
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gridExtra) # plotting multipanels 
```

```{r single scenarios}
# Define parasite density range
x <- seq(0.01, 100, length.out = 1000)

# Define grooming scenarios 

## no grooming 
death_none <- function(x) rep(0, length(x))

## programmed to a certain number of fleas per day (G)
death_programmed <-  function(x, G = 10) {
  pmin(1, G / x)  # cap at 1 to avoid unrealistic death rates > 1
} 

## saturate to a % of fleas (e.g., stimulus driven) perG
death_stimulus <- function(x, d0 = 0, dmax = 1, perG  = 0.75) {
  k <- x * (1 - perG) / perG
  d0 + (dmax * x) / (k + x)
}

## costly, stimulus driven to a certain point, but then there is a cost 
death_costly <- function(x, max = 1, mu = 10, sigma = 10) {
  max * exp(-((x - mu)^2) / (2 * sigma^2))
}

# Evaluate each function
df <- data.frame(
  x = x,
  none = death_none(x),
  programmed = death_programmed(x),
  stimulus = death_stimulus(x),
  costly = death_costly(x)
)


df_long <- pivot_longer(df, -x, names_to = "grooming scenario", values_to = "DeathRate")

# Plot
## Facet plot of grooming scenarios 
ggplot(df_long, aes(x = x, y = DeathRate, color = `grooming scenario`)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~factor(`grooming scenario`, levels = c("none", "programmed", "stimulus", "costly")))  +
  labs(
    x = "ectoparasite population size",
    y = expression(mu)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") + 
  theme(legend.position = "none")

## Merge plot of grooming scenarios 
  
```


```{r continuous parameter space }
# plot different grooming scenarios 

# 1. Define parameter ranges
x_vals <- seq(1, 100, length.out = 100)  # parasite population size 
G_vals <- seq(1, 100, length.out = 100)   # grooming efficiency

# 2. Create a grid of all combinations
grid <- expand.grid(
  pop_size = x_vals,
  groom_efficiency = G_vals
)

# 3. Define total groomed for each scenario

# Programmed: can remove up to G fleas per day
grid$programmed <- pmin(grid$pop_size, grid$groom_efficiency)
grid$programmed_rate <- grid$programmed/ grid$pop_size

# Stimulus-driven: can remove up to %perG per day 
k_vals <-
grid$stimulus <- grid$pop_size*grid$groom_efficiency/100 # thinking if saturating at 0.75% 

# Costly: Gaussian centered at mu = 50, sigma = 15
mu <- 50
sigma <- 15
efficiency <- exp(-((grid$pop_size - mu)^2) / (2 * sigma^2))
grid$costly <- grid$death_rate * efficiency

# 4. Reshape for plotting
plot_data <- pivot_longer(grid, cols = c("programmed", "stimulus", "costly"),
                          names_to = "scenario", values_to = "total_groomed")

plot_data$scenario <- factor(plot_data$scenario,
                             levels = c("programmed", "stimulus", "costly"))

# 5. Plot faceted heatmap
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = pop_size, y = death_rate, fill = total_groomed)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "inferno", direction = -1) +
  facet_wrap(~scenario) +
  labs(
    x = "ectoparasite population size",
    y = "grooming efficiency",
    fill = "mortality rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
# plot different grooming scenarios 

# 1. Define parameter ranges
x_vals <- seq(1, 100, length.out = 100)  # parasite population size 
G_vals <- seq(1, 100, length.out = 100)   # grooming efficiency

# 2. Create a grid of all combinations
grid_prog <- expand.grid(
  pop_size = x_vals,
  groom_efficiency = G_vals
)

# Provide some columns for 
## scenario 
grid_prog$scenario <- grid_prog$groom_efficiency

# Programmed: can remove up to G fleas per day
grid_prog$fleas_groomed <- pmin(grid_prog$pop_size, grid_prog$groom_efficiency)
grid_prog$death_rate <- grid_prog$fleas_groomed/ grid_prog$pop_size

# PROGRAMMED
## Plot full parameter space for Programmed 
ggplot(grid_prog, aes(x = pop_size, y = groom_efficiency, fill = death_rate)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "inferno", direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "ectoparasite population size",
    y = "programmed groom limit",
    fill = "mortality rate"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

## PROGRAMMED
# Plot example for all grooming efficiency scenarios 
prog_scenarios <- ggplot(grid_prog, aes(x = pop_size, y = death_rate, 
                      group = groom_efficiency, color = fleas_groomed)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_color_gradientn(
    colours = c("gray70", viridis::viridis(255, option = "inferno", direction = -1)),
    values  = scales::rescale(c(0, 100)),
    limits  = c(0, 100),
    breaks  = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100),
    name    = "ectoparasite deaths"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "ectoparasite population size",
    y = "death rate",
    title = expression(bold("a.") ~ " Programmed")
  ) +
  theme_classic()
prog_scenarios # check all good

## make a no legend version for multi-panel
prog_scenarios_nl <- prog_scenarios + 
  theme(legend.position = "none")


# Stimulus-driven: can remove up to %perG per day 
stim_rates <- seq(0.01, 1, length.out = 100)  # death rates (for stimulus)

grid_stim <- expand.grid(
  pop_size = x_vals,
  death_rate = stim_rates
)
grid_stim$scenario <- as.integer(factor(grid_stim$death_rate))
grid_stim$fleas_groomed <- floor(grid_stim$pop_size*grid_stim$death_rate)

## Plot full parameter space for Stimulus
ggplot(grid_stim, aes(x = pop_size, y = fleas_groomed, fill = death_rate)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "inferno", direction = -1) +
  labs(
    x = "ectoparasite population size",
    y = "grooming efficiency",
    fill = "mortality rate"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

stim_scenarios <- ggplot(grid_stim, aes(x = pop_size, y = death_rate, 
                      group = death_rate, color =  fleas_groomed)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_color_gradientn(
    colours = c("gray70", viridis::viridis(255, option = "inferno", direction = -1)),
    values  = scales::rescale(c(0, 100)),
    limits  = c(0, 100),
    breaks  = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100),
    name    = "Ectoparasite Deaths"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "ectoparasite population size",
    y = "death rate",
    title = expression(bold("b.") ~ " Stimulus-driven")
  ) +
  theme_classic()
stim_scenarios # check all good 

## make a no legend version for multi-panel
stim_scenarios_nl_ny <- stim_scenarios + 
  theme(legend.position = "none", 
        axis.title.y = element_blank(),
        axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

# Costly: Gaussian centered at a variable peak/optimal ectoparasite that then decreases 

## explore 100 different scenarios of cost 
cost_scenarios <- data.frame(
  scenario = 1:100,
  mu = runif(100, 10, 50),
  sigma = runif(100, 5, 25)
)

grid_cost  <- expand_grid(
  pop_size = 1:100, # ectoparasite population size
  scenario = 1:100 # this changes the type of gaussian curve
) 

grid_cost <- left_join(grid_cost, cost_scenarios, by = "scenario")
grid_cost$death_rate <- exp(-((grid_cost$pop_size - grid_cost$mu)^2) / (2 * grid_cost$sigma^2))
grid_cost$fleas_groomed <- floor(grid_cost$pop_size*grid_cost$death_rate)


cost_scenarios <- ggplot(grid_cost, aes(x = pop_size, y = death_rate, 
                      group = scenario, color =  fleas_groomed)) +
  geom_line() +
  scale_color_gradientn(
    colours = c("gray70", viridis::viridis(255, option = "inferno", direction = -1)),
    values  = scales::rescale(c(0, 100)),
    limits  = c(0, 100),
    breaks  = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100),
    name    = "ectoparasite deaths"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "ectoparasite population size",
    y = "death rate",
   title = expression(bold("c.") ~ " Costly")
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = c(0.98, 0.98),
        legend.justification = c(1, 1), 
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
        legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA), 
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 6),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 7)) + 
  guides(
  color = guide_colorbar(
    title.position = "top",
    title.hjust = 1, 
    barheight = unit(0.8, "in"),
    barwidth  = unit(0.2, "in"),
    ticks = FALSE))
cost_scenarios # check all good 

# remove y axis for multipanel
cost_scenarios_ny <- cost_scenarios + 
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(),
  axis.text.y  = element_blank(),
  axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

# plot all three scenarios next to eachother 
png(filename="figures/groom_scenarios.png", width = 7.5, height =3, unit = 'in', res = 300)
grid.arrange(prog_scenarios_nl, stim_scenarios_nl_ny, cost_scenarios_ny, nrow = 1)
dev.off()
```



```{r}
## need to make Grooming rates for much larger parasite populations 

# 1. Define parameter ranges
fm_x_vals <- seq(0, 1000, length.out = 1001)  # parasite population size 
G_vals <- seq(1, 100, length.out = 100)   # grooming efficiency

# 2. Create a grid of all combinations
grid_prog_fm <- expand.grid(
  pop_size = fm_x_vals,
  groom_efficiency = G_vals
)

# Provide some columns for 
## scenario 
grid_prog_fm$scenario <- grid_prog_fm$groom_efficiency

# Programmed: can remove up to G fleas per day
grid_prog_fm$fleas_groomed <- pmin(grid_prog_fm$pop_size, grid_prog_fm$groom_efficiency)
grid_prog_fm$death_rate <- grid_prog_fm$fleas_groomed/ grid_prog_fm$pop_size

# Stimulus-driven: can remove up to %perG per day 
grid_stim_fm <- expand.grid(
  pop_size = fm_x_vals,
  death_rate = stim_rates
)
grid_stim_fm$scenario <- as.integer(factor(grid_stim_fm$death_rate))
grid_stim_fm$fleas_groomed <- floor(grid_stim_fm$pop_size*grid_stim_fm$death_rate)


# Costly: Gaussian centered at a variable peak/optimal ectoparasite that then decreases 

## explore 100 different scenarios of cost 
cost_scenarios_fm <- data.frame(
  scenario = 1:100,
  mu = runif(100, 10, 50),
  sigma = runif(100, 5, 25)
)

grid_cost_fm  <- expand_grid(
  pop_size = 0:1000, # ectoparasite population size
  scenario = 1:100 # this changes the type of gaussian curve
) 

grid_cost_fm <- left_join(grid_cost_fm, cost_scenarios_fm, by = "scenario")
grid_cost_fm$death_rate <- exp(-((grid_cost_fm$pop_size - grid_cost_fm$mu)^2) / (2 * grid_cost_fm$sigma^2))
grid_cost_fm$fleas_groomed <- floor(grid_cost_fm$pop_size*grid_cost_fm$death_rate)

# saving grooming scenarios for use in the model 
grid_prog_fm$groom_type <- "programmed"
grid_prog_fm <- grid_prog_fm[, c("groom_type", "scenario", "pop_size", "death_rate")]
grid_stim_fm$groom_type <- "stimulus"
grid_stim_fm <- grid_stim_fm[, c("groom_type", "scenario", "pop_size", "death_rate")]
grid_cost_fm$groom_type <- "costly"
grid_cost_fm <- grid_cost_fm[, c("groom_type", "scenario", "pop_size", "death_rate")]

grooming_deathrates <- rbind(grid_prog_fm, grid_stim_fm, grid_cost_fm)
write.csv(grooming_deathrates, file = "data/grooming_deathrates.csv")
```
